title: Workstations
description: Choose and configure you-agent-factory workstations across two independent axes: WorkstationType (runtime implementation) and WorkstationKind behavior (scheduling), with a type-versus-behavior compatibility matrix and shared-field summary.
openingSummary: A workstation binds a named worker, owns step routing and limits, and combines a runtime type with a scheduling behavior. Pick both axes independently — especially keep type POLLER_RUN distinct from behavior POLLER — then open the matching variant page.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Use the type and behavior selection tables to open the matching variant pages. WorkstationType (field type) selects the runtime implementation family such as INFERENCE_RUN or AGENT_RUN. WorkstationKind (field behavior) selects scheduling such as STANDARD, REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER. The two axes combine freely: every published type is compatible with every published behavior. POLLER_RUN is a runtime type; POLLER is a scheduling behavior — they are not the same discriminator. Author shared fields such as name, worker, inputs, type, behavior, outputs, onFailure, and limits before variant-specific configuration.

selectType.title: Select A Runtime Type
selectType.body: Use the comparison below to open the matching WorkstationType page. Choose the runtime implementation that matches how the step should execute, then pick a behavior on the scheduling axis.

selectBehavior.title: Select A Scheduling Behavior
selectBehavior.body: Use the comparison below to open the matching WorkstationKind behavior page. Behavior controls when and how the step becomes ready; it does not replace the runtime type.

typeBehaviorMatrix.title: Type Versus Behavior Compatibility
typeBehaviorMatrix.body: Type and behavior are independent. Every cell below is a valid pairing: choose any WorkstationType with any WorkstationKind. The matrix is selection guidance, not a second schema contract.

sharedFields.title: Shared Fields
sharedFields.body: These fields apply across Workstation variants. Variant pages add type-specific or behavior-specific fields such as classificationRoutes, guards, cron, or model operation bindings. The live Workstation schema embed below shows the base contract from the installed Factory package.

workstationSchema.title: Workstation Schema
workstationSchema.body: The embed shows the live Factory Workstation base definition from the installed package. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this index.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This index is a Workstation family overview and selection surface. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not a Worker configuration guide, and not a full per-variant field dump. Variant pages own discriminator-specific configuration. Worker companion pages are discovery links only. Classifier workstations replace ordinary success and rejection outputs with classificationRoutes.

links.selectionInferenceRunWhen: Dispatch one-shot inference harness work through an inference worker
links.selectionAgentRunWhen: Dispatch agent-loop work through an agent worker with optional OpenCode overrides
links.selectionScriptRunWhen: Dispatch command-backed work through a script worker
links.selectionPollerRunWhen: Execute poller-worker runtime work — distinct from scheduling behavior POLLER
links.selectionModelWorkstationWhen: Model-oriented prompts, outcome formats, output schemas, and stop words
links.selectionModelInvokeWhen: Provider-neutral operation bindings and model operations
links.selectionLogicalMoveWhen: Non-worker logical routing with optional guarded loop breakers
links.selectionClassifierWhen: Classification routes with classifier-specific output restrictions
links.selectionStandardWhen: Ordinary readiness, dispatch, concurrency, and routing
links.selectionRepeaterWhen: Change-triggered looping and rejection reloop semantics
links.selectionCronWhen: Cron configuration, time work, guards, and input readiness
links.selectionPollerWhen: Long-lived poller scheduling — distinct from runtime type POLLER_RUN
links.pollerAxesNote: Do not collapse POLLER_RUN (runtime type) with POLLER (scheduling behavior). Configure both axes when a poller step needs poller-worker execution and poller scheduling.
links.sharedNameRole: Workstation identity declared in the factory topology
links.sharedWorkerRole: Named worker the workstation invokes at runtime
links.sharedInputsRole: Input readiness contract for the step
links.sharedTypeRole: WorkstationType discriminator for the runtime implementation
links.sharedOutputsRole: Success routing targets (classifier variants use classificationRoutes instead)
links.sharedOnFailureRole: Failure routing target shared across variants
links.sharedLimitsRole: Capacity and concurrency bounds for the step
